loop_cards.who_has: The lossless method that stores repeated patterns once and references them
Compression where data is permanently removed and cannot be recovered
The one-way function that produces a fixed-length digest
The encryption type that uses the same key to encrypt and decrypt
The encryption type that uses a public and private key pair
The attribute(s) that uniquely identify each record in a table
The primary key of another table used to link tables together
The process of organising data to reduce redundancy and anomalies
The rule requiring no partial dependencies on the whole key
The properties guaranteeing reliable transactions: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability
The network where data is split into packets routed independently and reassembled
The system that translates domain names into IP addresses
The device that connects different networks together using IP addresses
The device that connects devices within a single LAN using MAC addresses
The algorithm that ranks web pages by importance based on incoming links
The lossless method that replaces runs of repeated values with a value and a count

callers.clue: Compression where the original can be recovered exactly.
Compression that permanently removes data, used for JPEG and MP3.
Replaces runs of repeated values with a value and a count.
Stores repeated patterns once and references them; the index must be sent too.
Encryption using the same key to encrypt and decrypt.
Encryption using a public and private key pair.
One-way function producing a fixed-length value; used to store passwords.
Attribute(s) that uniquely identify each record.
The primary key of another table, used to link tables.
A primary key made from two or more attributes combined.
Organising data to reduce redundancy and remove anomalies.
The four properties of a reliable transaction.
Locking a record during a transaction to prevent the lost update problem.
A network covering a small area on a single site, hardware owned by one organisation.
A network covering a large geographic area, using third-party infrastructure.
Data split into independently routed units, reassembled at the destination.
Translates domain names into IP addresses.
Connects different networks together using IP addresses.
Connects devices within a LAN using MAC addresses.
Ranks web pages by importance using incoming links.

quickfire.clue: The lossless compression best suited to images with long runs of identical pixels.
The encryption weakness that asymmetric encryption solves by sharing a key openly.
The property of a hash function meaning the same input always gives the same output.
The normal form that removes transitive dependencies.
The table used to resolve a many-to-many relationship.
The ACID property meaning a transaction is all-or-nothing.
The rule that a foreign key must reference a valid existing primary key.
The SQL keyword that filters which rows are returned.
The SQL clause that combines columns from related tables on a matching key.
The TCP/IP layer that adds source and destination IP addresses.
The protocol used only for sending email.
JavaScript form validation runs in this location, which is why it is not secure on its own.
